item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite enjoy this one, partly because of the fun, whimsical tone, and partly because… ok, it was mostly just how amusing this concept is! If I had to make an improvement, I might make it more clear how the D-class figure breaks good Dr. Whatsisname's nose, because the sentence right before his nose gets broken describes the figurine flying towards the ceiling. Otherwise, very well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't actually have anyone in mind, It was just a random amount of black boxes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorites, creative as hell and now I really want it to be a thing, albeit less anamolous. I ca see it now, the Foundation following master Yogurt's sound advice: "Merchandising, merchandising, merchandising!"

Anyway, +1

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I agree. Correction made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, but I think I noticed a minor grammar mistake.

The ball will be placed in one instance of SCP-2228 until SCP-2228's anomalous effect manifests. The experiment will be repeated on two more instances of SCP-2228 to determine if any patterns can be observed in these manifestations.

Results: Shorty after introduction into the first instance of SCP-2228, the ball began to levitate 5cm off of the floor.

I'm pretty sure that was supposed to be "Shortly"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't put it past Dr. W to include a tiny jar of 447 as one of the sold-separately accessories or as the core of an SCP Foundation Containment Breach Response Unit playset.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My knee-jerk reaction was Rik Mayall, but then I realized there weren't enough letters, so Ian McKellen, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like a mini-173 who tries to snap your toes, along with a mini-682 who just tries to bite them off. They wouldn't get along very well though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sure it's been suggested before, I would totally buy this. If only it came with mini figures of popular objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The actor playing "Doctor Wondertainment" in this commercial has been identified as ███ ████████.

Hummm… who might he be? :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want this playset! Like, take all my money, man, where can I buy this thing?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is rough, but I found it to be very charming. I'd cut the test logs though, the article should end right after the commercial. Short and sweet. I'm upvoting for enjoying it up to the commercial though because I really liked that. It's very true to the Wondertainment theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How much would it cost to buy one of these?
+1, this is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. Is. Amazing. Seriously, this was a stroke of brilliance and hilarity!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. In universe LOL-foundation from Dr. Wondertainment. Serious upvotage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvote!

I totally Like the Idea…and I would have some MiniSCPlab too xD
Was highly entertaining to read the Article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
hour of anomalous fun!

You got me there pal. Now i wish to know what the heck is a GOC Green Termination

+1

PD1: I want that playset.
PD2: Foundation is probably jelly, and want their copyrights

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually it would be equivalent to 1.44g forces, because the bones would have twice the surface area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh gosh this is rad
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, they're action figures moving on their own, so it might look something like robot chicken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it wrong that my envisioning of the experiments happened in claymation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I felt exactly the opposite; the commercial I could take or leave, but the testing logs were a highly entertaining read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't catch that. Fixed now, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is fantastic. I like how it didn't just show up somewhere like most Wondertainment stuff does. I also appreciate that the Foundation has finally learned that to take Doctor W.'s safety warnings seriously.

as well as sudden declarations to a stationary position

"Woah, horsey!"

That was a fun mental image but you probably meant 'decelerations'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my favorite skips of the year. The ""Global Occult Coalition Type Green Termination Playset" is fantastic.

(FYI: If you can replace it with "it is" and the sentence still makes sense, the word is "it's". Otherwise, "its".)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this article but feel the testing logs are not the least bit necessary. Like at all. The advertisement is enough. Neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
insta-anomalizer

I can feel the Wondertainment in this one. I love good Wondertainment articles, so you have earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1 10x15cm sheet of paper containing information on an upcoming "Global Occult Coalition Type Green Termination Playset"

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well written Dr. Wondertainment products never fail to put a nerdy grin on my face. I think my favorite bit of this were the many deaths of the d-class figure, and the strange revenge moment where it broke the researcher's nose. I like to think that a bunch of D-class smiled at that moment without knowing quite why.

Also, this occurred to me later on yesterday after reading this: a rat tripling in volume could still land within square-cube tolerance for a rat. It might not be a healthy giant rat, but 3 g-forces worth of illusory mass (the best analogy we have to actually tripling a creature in size) is considered well within safe limits for both jerk and sustained acceleration. Did you maybe mean tripling in dimensions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can very quickly see this becoming one of my favorite Wondertainment skips. This is so charming and almost magical in its presentation/execution, yet still clinical and scientific and exactly how I'd imagine the Foundation approaching this. I feel nostalgic for some playsets…

+1

One thing though: did you mean to tag the GOC instead of the CI?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Funny story about that…

The real reason I did it was because I couldn't think of what would happen with those two.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been a while since I've seen a Wondertainment skip, and this is a really good one.

Also:

Due to the limited value of the information that can be gained from this experiment, Site 73 command has deemed the testing of these two items an unnecessary risk

Thank you. Thank you thank you thank you. I don't know how many SCPs I've seen on the site that do stupid shit like this, and it is very refreshing to have the Foundation say "…you know what? Maybe that's a bad idea!" and not do it. Thank you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, but who is to say that there is only one Wondertainment?

I like how you pulled it off with the actor, though, that was neat.

Also this is fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this after watching The LEGO Movie, during which I got the idea of a Foundation LEGO set. As a result, I cannot get the image of this as a LEGO set out of my head. However, I like the idea of the kindly Doctor W. casually lifting the Veil for the sake of making a children's playset.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. This idea came to me after watching a toy commercial, so I knew I had to put one in here. Having Wonderment show up sounded like a logical choice, but I knew everyone would hate it if it was actually him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a little worried when Dr. Wondertainment showed up in the commercial, but I liked that you went out of your way to specifically note that it was an actor, and probably didn't represent the actual Dr. Wondertainment. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The SCP Foundation, SCP Foundation logo, and "Secure, Contain, Protect" are registered trademarks of the SCP Foundation. All rights reserved."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My horsey can teleport now!

I'm getting some traumatic flashbacks to my first ever scip.

Curse you LordMetalton! Curse you!

+1
